name: AWS Activate

stacking.notes: Cannot stack multiple Activate tiers simultaneously; can apply new credits when prior expire

notes: Priority service for ML/AI workloads via Bedrock, SageMaker. Check current quota on model training credits.

name: Google Cloud for Startups

stacking.notes: Can stack Spark + Ignite if progression; Workspace for Startups can be added separately

notes: Gemini API credits often available separately via AI Startup Program at cloud.google.com/ai-startups

name: Microsoft for Startups (Azure + M365)

stacking.notes: GitHub Advanced Security + Copilot often bundled; stack with LinkedIn Learning 25 seats

notes: Azure OpenAI Service access included â€” high value for AI-native products. GitHub Copilot often bundled.

name: Stripe Atlas Perks

stacking.notes: Atlas perks are additive to all other programs. AWS $1K Atlas perk stacks with AWS Activate Founders tier.

notes: Total perk value often 10-20x the $500 Atlas fee. Retroactive access for existing Atlas companies. Perks include AWS $1K, GCP $2K, HubSpot starter free, Gusto 6mo free, Carta $1.5K, 15+ others.

name: Y Combinator Startup School

stacking.notes: Startup School deals are subset of full YC batch. Can stack SUS deals with independent applications.

notes: Free enrollment. Unlocks partial deal set including AWS $25K, Brex $75K, Notion free, Stripe waived fees even without batch acceptance.

name: Brex Startup Rewards

stacking.notes: Brex perks are additive. Brex AWS credits $1K-$5K stack with AWS Activate. Check both Brex and Ramp portals for overlapping deals.

notes: Anthropic API credits often available via Brex. 7x points on software, 3x on travel. OpenAI/Anthropic credits especially valuable for AI products.

name: Vercel Startup Program

stacking.notes: Can stack with Next.js hosting credits if building Next.js apps. Pairs well with Supabase for full-stack JAMStack.

notes: Pairs well with Supabase startup credits for full-stack JAMStack builds. Apply when deploying first production app.

name: Anthropic Claude API Credits

stacking.notes: Credits from different sources applied sequentially. Combine with OpenAI for multi-provider AI coverage.

notes: Fastest path: Brex account (instant via portal). Watch for AI-focused accelerator cohort perks.

name: Notion for Startups

stacking.notes: Independent from cloud credits; free to stack

notes: 6 months free Plus plan then 50% off first full year. Good for ops documentation. Referral via YC/Brex/Stripe Atlas often speeds approval.

name: Supabase Startup Program

stacking.notes: Additive with Vercel startup credits for JAMStack stack

notes: Full Pro plan: daily backups, email support, 8GB database, 100GB storage. Apply before first production deploy.

name: HubSpot for Startups

stacking.notes: Cannot stack additional HubSpot credits; one discount tier at a time

notes: Critical for outbound sales infrastructure. Integration with Stripe and LinkedIn. Apply at pre-revenue for maximum discount year.

name: Linear Startup Program

stacking.notes: Additive with GitHub, Notion, Vercel credits

notes: Fastest approval of any program. Full Plus plan: unlimited issues, cycles, roadmaps. Free GitHub integration.

name: Cloudflare for Startups

stacking.notes: Additive with cloud infrastructure credits (AWS/GCP/Azure do not overlap)

notes: Full Business plan: CDN, DDoS mitigation, Workers (10M req/day), R2 storage (10GB), Pages, Zero Trust. Zero Trust free up to 50 users.

name: DigitalOcean Hatch

stacking.notes: Does not conflict with AWS/GCP/Azure. Can run DO workloads in parallel.

notes: ~30% acceptance rate. Stronger application with deployed product. Best value for simple VPS/containerized workloads.

name: MongoDB Atlas for Startups

stacking.notes: Free M0 tier always available without application. Startup credits additive.

notes: Atlas App Services (serverless functions, triggers) highly relevant for automation workflows. M0 free tier available immediately.

name: Datadog for Startups

stacking.notes: Additive with cloud provider credits; operates independently

notes: Pro tier: Infrastructure monitoring (5 hosts), APM (500K traces), Logs (1GB/day). Faster with VC/accelerator endorsement.

name: Twilio Startup Program

stacking.notes: Additive with cloud provider credits; independent

notes: SendGrid (Twilio-owned) included â€” covers transactional email AND SMS in single credit pool.

name: Amplitude Analytics for Startups

stacking.notes: Complements Segment (CDP). Do NOT run Amplitude + Mixpanel simultaneously â€” fragmented data.

notes: Choose Amplitude OR Mixpanel OR PostHog as primary analytics platform. Apply at first product launch.

name: GitHub for Startups

stacking.notes: Complements Microsoft for Startups (Azure). GitHub Copilot often bundled via Microsoft for Startups separately.

notes: 20 seats GitHub Team free 1yr. GitHub Actions 3K min/month. Copilot available at discounted/free rate via Microsoft for Startups bundle.

name: PostHog for Startups

stacking.notes: Pick PostHog OR Amplitude OR Mixpanel as primary analytics. PostHog self-hosted always free as fallback.

notes: All PostHog Cloud features: product analytics, session replay, feature flags, A/B testing, heatmaps, surveys. Self-hosted option always free at any scale.

name: Mercury for Startups

stacking.notes: Mercury is a banking rail, not a credit stack conflict. Run alongside Brex or Ramp for spend rewards.

notes: Free checking/savings, no minimum balance, free domestic/international wires. Mercury API enables payment automation hooks. Mercury Raise: curated access to 600+ VCs.

name: Figma for Startups

stacking.notes: Additive with all other credits; design is independent from infra/analytics stack

notes: Full Professional plan: unlimited files, version history, Dev Mode. FigJam included for architecture diagrams.

name: JetBrains for Startups

stacking.notes: Additive with all other credits

notes: All Products Pack: IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, DataGrip, WebStorm, GoLand, Rider. Renewable up to 3 years â€” unusually long.

name: OpenAI Startup Credits

stacking.notes: Stack with Anthropic credits for multi-provider AI coverage. Azure OpenAI is a separate billing path.

notes: No direct public startup program as of 2026. Access is partner-gated. Apply Microsoft for Startups first for Azure OpenAI access.

name: Ramp Corporate Card

stacking.notes: Run Ramp alongside Mercury banking. Brex and Ramp perks portals have different partner deals â€” check both.

notes: 1.5% cashback on all spend. No personal guarantee required. Perks include AWS, Stripe, Google Workspace, Gusto. Bill pay and accounting integrations.

name: Mixpanel for Startups

stacking.notes: Conflicts with Amplitude â€” choose one primary analytics platform. Segment as CDP complements Mixpanel.

notes: 100M monthly tracked events. User-level tracking strong for conversion funnel analysis. Choose Amplitude OR Mixpanel, not both.

name: Hugging Face for Startups

stacking.notes: Complements AWS/GCP AI credits. HF hosts open-source models while AWS/GCP provide inference infrastructure.

notes: Inference API, Spaces (GPU apps), AutoTrain (fine-tuning), private repos. Can replace paid API costs with open-source models.

name: Auth0 / Okta for Startups

stacking.notes: Additive with cloud credits; handles identity layer independently

notes: 7,500 MAU free. Social login, MFA, SSO, M2M tokens, attack protection. Saves months of custom auth development.

name: Segment for Startups

stacking.notes: Segment as CDP collects once and routes to any analytics tool. Non-conflicting with analytics platforms â€” layer Segment under them.

notes: Unlimited sources/destinations, data replay, S3. Collect data once, send anywhere. Pipe customer events into HubSpot, BigQuery automatically.

name: Intercom Early Stage Program

stacking.notes: Complements HubSpot (CRM/sales). Intercom handles customer-facing support and in-app messaging.

notes: 95% off Year 1 â€” one of the most generous discount structures in SaaS. Fin AI bot handles support tickets automatically. Verified via Crunchbase/LinkedIn.

name: Relay Business Banking

stacking.notes: Use Relay for treasury management alongside Mercury. Different partner perk networks â€” worth having both. Use Ramp or Brex on top for spend rewards.

notes: 20 sub-accounts + 50 virtual cards for project-level budget tracking. Free ACH/wires, QuickBooks/Xero/Gusto integrations. Use for operational accounts.

name: Visa Startup Partner Program

stacking.notes: Additive with all cloud/SaaS credits. Focused on payments infrastructure.

notes: Visa Direct API unlocks real-time payout capabilities. Strongest applications have live transaction volume. Mastercard has parallel programs (Foundry, StartPath).

name: Oracle for Startups (OCI Credits)

stacking.notes: Stacks cleanly with all major clouds. OCI Always Free tier (2 VMs, 200GB storage) is perpetual and separate from startup credits.

notes: OCI has a very generous Always Free tier that never expires. Startup program adds $10K-$300K on top. Autonomous Database free tier excellent for Postgres-compatible workloads.

name: Linode / Akamai Cloud Startup Program

stacking.notes: Stacks with all other cloud platforms. Simple self-serve pricing; great for testing multi-region deployments.

notes: Simple VPS platform. Nanode 1GB ($5/mo) is among the cheapest ways to host Python Flask apps. Credits stretch to 50 months of base hosting.

name: Fly.io Startup Credits

stacking.notes: Excellent direct Render replacement. Deploy Python/Flask containers globally via Dockerfile.

notes: Fly.io is the closest 1:1 replacement for Render for Python Flask apps. Supports Postgres, Redis, background workers. Free allowances on top of startup credits.

name: Cohere for Startups

stacking.notes: Stacks with all AI providers. Cohere Command and Embed models are distinct from OpenAI/Anthropic.

notes: Cohere specializes in enterprise NLP: Embed (search/RAG), Command (generation), Rerank (relevance). Strong for semantic search in BadgeTrack or document apps.

name: Groq Startup Program

stacking.notes: Groq LPU inference is fastest available (Llama 3, Mixtral). Stacks with any other AI provider.

notes: Groq is the fastest inference provider available at 500-800 tokens/sec on Llama 3. 10B+ token/month usage makes this high-value for Trading Agent and other AI-heavy builds.

name: Together AI for Startups

stacking.notes: Open-source model hosting platform. Stacks with all other AI APIs.

notes: Together AI runs Llama, Mistral, Qwen, and 100+ open-source models at low cost. Fine-tuning available. Good alternative when OpenAI/Anthropic budgets run out.

name: Replicate Startup Program

stacking.notes: Stacks with all other AI providers. Unique for image/video/audio generation models.

notes: Replicate specializes in image/video/audio generation models (Stable Diffusion, etc). Useful if apps need media generation capabilities.

name: Sentry for Startups

stacking.notes: Sentry (errors) stacks with Datadog/New Relic (metrics/APM). Different layers of observability.

notes: Sentry is the standard error monitoring tool for Python Flask apps. Deployed BadgeTrack product qualifies. SDK installs in 5 minutes. Python, JS, and all major languages supported.

name: Postman Startup Program

stacking.notes: Stacks cleanly with all dev tools.

notes: Postman Professional includes Collections, automated testing, mock servers, and API documentation hosting. Useful for building and testing the Runway API and perk-engine API.

name: CircleCI Startup Program

stacking.notes: CircleCI integrates with GitHub. Stack with GitHub for Startups for full CI/CD pipeline.

notes: CI/CD pipeline automation. Connects to GitHub repos, auto-runs tests and deploys on push. Python-native support.

name: LaunchDarkly Startup Program

stacking.notes: Feature flags integrate with deployment and monitoring pipelines.

notes: Feature flag management system. Enables safe rollouts and A/B testing without redeployments. Critical for the self-serve Runway product launch.

name: Neon Startup Program

stacking.notes: Neon is serverless Postgres. Pairs well with Vercel (Edge) or Fly.io. Can replace Render Postgres.

notes: Neon is serverless, branching Postgres - direct replacement for Render Postgres. Each git branch can have its own DB branch. BadgeTrack uses Postgres already, making migration straightforward.

name: SendGrid Transactional Email Credits

stacking.notes: SendGrid (transactional email API) stacks with Twilio (SMS/voice) and Customer.io (marketing automation).

notes: Twilio SendGrid: 40K free emails/day on free plan, startup program unlocks Essentials. Industry standard for transactional email. Python SDK available. Separate product from Twilio SMS.

name: Customer.io Startup Program

stacking.notes: Customer.io is marketing automation on top of event data. Works alongside SendGrid (transactional) and Segment (event collection).

notes: Customer.io handles lifecycle email marketing (onboarding, re-engagement, upsell). Best for driving app engagement once users sign up for any of the products being built.

name: Algolia for Startups

stacking.notes: Algolia search can be augmented with Cohere semantic embeddings. Stacks with any backend.

notes: Hosted search-as-a-service. Relevant for Runway program catalog search, BadgeTrack badge discovery, and Yard Sale listing search. Sub-100ms results.

name: SBIR / STTR Federal R&D Grants

stacking.notes: SBIR/STTR does not conflict with commercial startup programs. Many SBIR awardees also have AWS/GCP credits.

notes: Non-dilutive federal R&D grants. Phase I ~$275K, Phase II up to $1.75M. AI/ML-focused agencies (DoD, NSF, NIH) all fund software. Long cycle time (6 months) but highest non-dilutive value available. Trading Agent and Runway qualify as tech R&D.

name: Business Oregon Innovation Grants

stacking.notes: State grants stack with federal SBIR/STTR and all commercial programs.

notes: Oregon-based businesses qualify for Business Oregon grant programs. The Oregon SBDC also offers free consulting and grant-writing support. Nevada LLC with Oregon-based operations may qualify for some programs.

name: Square for Startups

stacking.notes: Square (in-person) stacks with Stripe (online). Use Square for physical commerce, Stripe for online.

notes: Square is ideal for junk removal (in-person tap-to-pay) and Airbnb add-ons. Can run alongside Stripe for different revenue streams. Square for Developers API is free.

name: New Relic Startup Program

stacking.notes: New Relic full-stack APM stacks with Sentry (error tracking) and can complement Datadog.

notes: New Relic free tier is very generous: 100GB/mo data ingest free, 1 free full-access user. Startup program adds credits on top. Full APM, logs, traces, and synthetic monitoring included.

name: Cloudinary Startup Program

stacking.notes: Cloudinary image/video CDN integrates with any web host.

notes: Cloudinary handles image/video upload, transformation, and CDN delivery. Relevant for Yard Sale app (product photos), Airbnb listings, and GetOutATown directory images.

name: Namecheap for Startups

stacking.notes: Domain registrar credits stack with Cloudflare DNS and Vercel hosting.

notes: Namecheap startup program provides domain registration credits. Multiple apps being built need domains. YC SUS members get additional discounts.

name: Oregon SBDC Network
provider: Oregon Small Business Development Center Network

tiers.name: Free Business Advising
tiers.description: Free one-on-one business counseling, training, and workshops from experienced advisors

tiers.name: Grant Application Support
tiers.description: Advisors help apply for Business Oregon, SBA Microloan, and federal SBIR/STTR grants â€” equivalent value in consulting fees saved

stacking.notes: SBDC advisors help access and stack other Oregon and federal programs. No monetary limit â€” stacks with everything.

notes: Free one-on-one business counseling and grant-writing support via 19 SBDC centers at Oregon community colleges and universities. Nevada LLC with Oregon-based operations qualifies â€” eligibility is based on business location not incorporation state. Advisors specifically help with Business Oregon grant applications and federal SBIR/STTR prep. No application required â€” schedule an appointment directly. High ROI: immediate, free, unlocks other programs.

name: RAIN Catalysts

tiers.name: Rural Venture Catalyst
tiers.description: Free workshops, mentorship, and business development support for rural Oregon entrepreneurs

tiers.name: Capital Access Program
tiers.description: Connections to angel investors and non-dilutive funding sources; some direct micro-grants via USDA-backed programming

stacking.notes: Non-dilutive; stacks with all commercial programs and other state/federal grants.

notes: RAIN (Regional Accelerator & Innovation Network) supports Oregon innovators â€” especially in underserved rural areas â€” through free workshops, venture catalyst connections, and capital access programs backed by USDA Rural Development grants. Nevada LLC with Oregon operations qualifies. Focused on Lane, Linn, Benton, Lincoln, and Grant counties but serves all of Oregon. RAIN can bridge connections to Portland Metro and statewide funding networks.

name: Oregon AI Accelerator
provider: Oregon AI Accelerator (Google + PSU/OSU/OHSU + Portland Metro Hub)

tiers.name: Cohort Participation
tiers.description: 3-month hybrid accelerator (Portland in-person + virtual); mentorship and validation without equity

tiers.name: Grant Prize
tiers.description: Equity-free grant prizes up to $50K awarded to top performers in the cohort

stacking.notes: Equity-free, non-dilutive; stacks with all cloud/AI credits and other Oregon programs. Google backing means potential GCP credit connections.

notes: Launched 2026 with Google seed funding. 20 early-stage AI startups per cohort (50%+ Oregon-based). 3-month hybrid program in Portland. Up to $50K in equity-free grant prizes. Run annually â€” applications typically open Nov/Dec for Feb cohort. Highly relevant for AI/ML apps being built. Board member building AI apps (trading agent, yard sale app etc.) is a strong fit. Applications from non-Oregon companies accepted if AI-focused.

name: Oregon Regional Innovation Hubs
provider: Business Oregon / Oregon Innovation Council (Oregon InC)

tiers.name: Hub Services
tiers.description: Free technical assistance, mentorship, networking, and talent development via 8 state-funded regional hubs

tiers.name: Capital Facilitation
tiers.description: Hubs facilitate access to state innovation grants and investor networks; some hubs manage direct grant programs

stacking.notes: Hubs are facilitators â€” they unlock other programs. Stacks with everything.

notes: Business Oregon and Oregon InC fund 8 regional innovation hubs statewide ($3.77M awarded 2025-2027 cycle). Hubs include Portland Metro Hub, Southern Oregon Innovation Hub, and others. Each hub provides technical assistance, investor connections, and manages local grant programs for high-growth tech companies. Nevada LLC with Oregon-based operations qualifies. Portland Metro Hub is the primary contact for Portland-area startups. Hubs also run the Oregon AI Accelerator program.

name: Oregon Entrepreneurial Development Loan Fund (EDLF)

tiers.name: Micro-Loan
tiers.description: Small business micro-loans for Oregon startups that can't access conventional financing

tiers.name: Growth Loan
tiers.description: Larger loan for established micro-enterprises and small businesses expanding in Oregon

stacking.notes: Loan (not grant) â€” does not reduce equity but must be repaid. Stacks with all grant and credit programs.

notes: Business Oregon's EDLF provides low-interest loans ($5K-$100K) to Oregon startups and micro-enterprises that can't access conventional bank financing. Not a grant â€” must be repaid â€” but below-market interest rates and flexible terms. Oregon SBDC advisors help with the application. Nevada LLC with Oregon operations qualifies if primary business activity is in Oregon. Good bridge financing while pursuing grants and cloud credits.

name: Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub

stacking.notes: Azure credits + GitHub Enterprise + Azure OpenAI. One of the most valuable all-in-one programs.

notes: Includes Azure credits, GitHub Enterprise, Microsoft 365, LinkedIn Premium, Azure OpenAI access.

name: OVH Cloud Startup Program

stacking.notes: Good for European data residency; GDPR-compliant

notes: Strong European cloud alternative. Bare metal and managed Kubernetes available.

name: Scaleway Startup Program

stacking.notes: Best for GPU-heavy workloads in Europe; H100 clusters available

notes: Strong GPU cloud option with H100 availability.

name: IBM Cloud for Startups

stacking.notes: Best for enterprise/compliance workloads; Watson AI APIs included

notes: Good for regulated industries. IBM Watson AI credits included.

name: Fastly Fast Forward Startup Program

stacking.notes: CDN + edge compute + WAF

notes: CDN, edge compute, WAF credits for early-stage startups.

name: Render Startup Program

stacking.notes: Best for teams already on Render (Python Flask); bridge before AWS migration

notes: For teams already using Render. Good bridge before migrating to AWS/GCP.

name: Railway Startup Program

stacking.notes: Simple deployment platform with GitHub integration

notes: Fast deploy platform popular with solo developers. GitHub-native workflow.

name: Hetzner Cloud Referral Credits

stacking.notes: Best price/performance for European VPS; dev/staging environments

notes: Extremely cost-competitive European VPS. Best price/performance for non-US workloads.

name: NVIDIA Inception Program

stacking.notes: Membership unlocks cloud partner discounts for GPU instances

notes: Free to join. Unlocks NVIDIA GPU cloud credits, cuDNN/TensorRT licenses, go-to-market support.

name: Mistral AI La Plateforme Startups

stacking.notes: Stack with other LLM providers for redundancy and cost arbitrage

notes: Access to Mistral Large and Codestral. Strong EU data compliance use cases.

name: Modal Labs Startup Credits

stacking.notes: Serverless GPU inference; pay-per-second billing

notes: Serverless GPU platform for Python. Great for AI inference APIs and ML pipelines.

name: Lambda Labs GPU Cloud Startup

stacking.notes: On-demand H100/A100 clusters for training and fine-tuning

notes: On-demand GPU cloud at competitive rates. Strong H100 availability for model training.

name: RunPod Startup Program

stacking.notes: Spot GPU instances at lowest cost

notes: Cheapest GPU spot instances. Excellent for cost-conscious AI inference.

name: Fireworks AI Startup Program

stacking.notes: Ultra-fast inference for open-source models

notes: Fast inference API for open-source models. Low-latency Llama, Mixtral hosting.

name: Anyscale Startup Program

stacking.notes: Ray-based distributed computing for complex ML pipelines

notes: Built on Ray framework. Best for distributed ML training and serving at scale.

name: Google AI Startup Program (Vertex AI / Gemini)

stacking.notes: Separate from GCP Spark credits; specifically for Gemini API / Vertex AI

notes: Google AI Startup Program with dedicated Gemini API and Vertex AI credits. Separate from GCP startup credits.

name: GitLab for Startups

stacking.notes: GitLab Ultimate includes CI/CD, SAST/DAST, container registry

notes: Free GitLab Ultimate. Advanced CI/CD, security scanning, container/package registries.

name: Atlassian Startups Program

stacking.notes: Jira + Confluence + Bitbucket bundle; can run alongside GitHub

notes: Free Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket for 10 users Year 1. 50% off Year 2.

name: Snyk for Startups

stacking.notes: Automated dependency and container scanning

notes: Free Team plan. Scans code, containers, IaC for vulnerabilities.

name: Retool Startup Program

stacking.notes: Connects to any database; stack with backend DB credits

notes: Build internal dashboards. Connects to Postgres, Mongo, REST APIs.

name: Grafana for Startups

stacking.notes: Grafana Cloud for custom dashboards + Datadog for APM

notes: Grafana Cloud startup plan: metrics, logs, traces, dashboards.

name: Webflow Startup Program

stacking.notes: Marketing site + CMS with HubSpot integration

notes: No-code website builder. Free Business plan. Good for marketing sites without engineering overhead.

name: Doppler Startup Program

stacking.notes: Universal secrets manager; integrates with all major cloud providers

notes: Universal secrets manager. Syncs environment variables across cloud providers and team members.

name: Buildkite Startup Program

stacking.notes: Hybrid CI/CD with self-hosted agents; faster for heavy workloads

notes: Self-hosted CI/CD pipelines. Faster and cheaper than cloud CI for heavy workloads. Startup year free.

name: Zendesk for Startups

stacking.notes: Choose one primary support tool

notes: Free Zendesk Suite for 6 months. Ticketing, live chat, knowledge base.

name: Freshworks Startup Program

stacking.notes: Full Freshworks suite: Freshdesk + Freshsales + Freshchat

notes: Full suite free Year 1. Great value bundle for solo founders.

name: Miro Startup Program

stacking.notes: Whiteboarding pairs well with Jira/Linear

notes: Free Miro Team plan. Visual collaboration and whiteboarding.

name: Hotjar for Startups

stacking.notes: Session recordings + heatmaps complement event analytics

notes: Free Business plan: session recordings, heatmaps, feedback widgets. Essential for UX optimization.

name: Pendo Startup Program

stacking.notes: In-app guides + analytics; complements Intercom for onboarding

notes: Product analytics plus in-app guides and NPS. Great for B2B SaaS onboarding.

name: Braze for Startups

stacking.notes: Multi-channel marketing automation; stack with Segment for data

notes: Enterprise customer engagement: email, push, SMS, in-app. High value for consumer apps.

name: Pipedrive Startup Program

stacking.notes: Lightweight sales CRM for SMB/solo founders alongside HubSpot marketing

notes: Sales pipeline CRM. Free Professional year. Simple, visual pipeline management.

name: Arc Startup Banking & Treasury

stacking.notes: Treasury and banking; stack with Ramp for expense management

notes: Startup banking with treasury management and revenue-based financing options.

name: Wise Business for Startups

stacking.notes: Best for international payments; stack with Mercury for US banking

notes: Best-in-class international business payments. Low FX fees for international contractors/vendors.

name: Capchase Revenue-Based Financing

stacking.notes: Revenue-based advance; requires existing recurring revenue

notes: Non-dilutive capital against ARR. Requires proven revenue. Great alternative to equity for SaaS.

name: Plaid Startup Program

stacking.notes: Bank account linking API; essential for fintech apps

notes: Discounted API costs for bank account linking. Critical for fintech, personal finance, and lending apps.

name: NSF I-Corps Program

stacking.notes: I-Corps gateway to NSF SBIR; completion improves acceptance rates

notes: Customer discovery training + stipend ($3K-$50K). Completion unlocks NSF SBIR. Completely non-dilutive.

name: NSF SBIR Phase I

stacking.notes: NSF SBIR does not preclude other agency SBIRs

notes: Most valuable non-dilutive grant for tech startups. $275K Phase I, up to $2M Phase II. No equity. ~6 month application.

name: Department of Energy SBIR/STTR

stacking.notes: Cleantech/energy focus; can stack across agencies on different R&D topics

notes: DOE SBIR for energy, climate, and advanced manufacturing. Active agency with many open solicitations.

name: NIH SBIR/STTR Program

stacking.notes: Health/biotech focus

notes: NIH SBIR for health, biotech, and medical devices. Highly competitive but large awards.

name: SBA 8(a) Business Development Program

stacking.notes: 8(a) unlocks sole-source federal contracts up to $4M

notes: SBA 8(a) for disadvantaged businesses. Unlocks sole-source federal contracts. Complex application but high long-term value.

name: California IBank Innovation Hub Programs
provider: California Office of Small Business Advocate

stacking.notes: State supplement to federal programs

notes: California IBank loan guarantees and Innovation Hub programs for CA-based startups.

name: New York State START-UP NY

stacking.notes: Requires NY university partnership; 10-year tax exemption

notes: 10-year tax exemption for startups on/near NY state university campuses.

name: USDA SBIR/STTR Program

stacking.notes: AgTech/food/rural tech focus; less competitive than NSF/NIH

notes: USDA SBIR for agricultural tech, food systems, and rural development.

name: Techstars Startup Toolkit (Alumni Perks)

stacking.notes: Techstars acceptance unlocks $250K+ in partner perks

notes: Techstars portfolio: $250K+ in partner perks including AWS, Google, Azure, HubSpot. Requires Techstars acceptance.

name: 500 Global Portfolio Startup Perks

stacking.notes: 500 Global portfolio perks include major cloud and SaaS discounts

notes: 500 Global portfolio: $100K+ in partner perks. Strong MENA, SEA, LATAM networks.

name: Pioneer.app Tournament

stacking.notes: Monthly competitive ranking; winners get cash + perks

notes: Monthly online tournament. Top 10 get $10K cash grants + perks. Free to enter.

name: Salesforce for Startups

stacking.notes: Salesforce best for B2B with 5+ salespeople

notes: Salesforce Startup Program: discounted/free licenses for early-stage B2B.

name: Slack for Startups

stacking.notes: Also available via accelerator bundles (YC, Techstars)

notes: Free Slack Pro for eligible startups. Also obtained through accelerator bundles.

name: DocuSign for Startups

stacking.notes: Essential for contractor agreements, NDAs, customer contracts

notes: Free Business Pro plan. E-signatures, templates, workflow automation for contracts.

name: Shopify Partner Program

stacking.notes: Partners earn 20% recurring revenue share on referred merchants

notes: Free developer stores. Partners earn 20% recurring revenue share. Good affiliate income stream.

name: Loom for Startups

stacking.notes: Async video messaging; integrates with Atlassian suite

notes: Free Loom Business for startups. Async screen recordings for team communication and demos.

name: Meta Business Startup Program

stacking.notes: Ad credits are one-time; stack with Google Ads for multi-channel acquisition

notes: Meta ad credits for Facebook/Instagram campaigns. One-time grant for qualifying early-stage startups.

name: Intel Liftoff for Startups

stacking.notes: Intel Gaudi AI accelerators; growing alternative to NVIDIA GPU compute

notes: Access to Intel Gaudi AI accelerators, developer tools, go-to-market support.

name: Stripe Startup Discount

stacking.notes: Processing fee discounts + dedicated support; separate from Stripe Atlas

notes: Stripe startup: processing fee discounts and dedicated support. Apply via partner accelerators for best access.

name: CNCF Startup Credits (Cloud Native)

stacking.notes: CNCF membership unlocks credits across cloud partners; Kubernetes-ecosystem focus

notes: CNCF Sandbox and member programs for cloud-native infrastructure.

name: Open Collective Fiscal Sponsorship

stacking.notes: Legal entity + donation processing for open-source projects

notes: Fiscal sponsorship for open-source projects. Tax-deductible donations without forming an LLC.

name: Hugging Face Pro Startup Credits

stacking.notes: HF Inference Endpoints startup credits; stack with compute for fine-tuning

notes: Hugging Face Pro startup credits for Inference Endpoints and Spaces compute.

name: Vercel AI Startup Program

stacking.notes: Vercel AI SDK credits for LLM streaming and AI-powered Next.js apps

notes: Vercel AI-specific startup program with enhanced credits for AI-powered applications.

name: Cloudflare Workers for Startups

stacking.notes: Workers, D1, R2, KV edge compute credits
